user_prompt: Configure it with a sprite, the sprite dimensions, the sprite's position will be automatically determined for being at the top of the view (avoiding the top bar) and centered.
Configuration also sets a text to be displayed.

Configuration also sets a "next view" to transition to when dismissed. Sometimes it will be the previous view, sometimes it will be the home view. Since configuring happens before showing the view, the caller can easily just use `getCurrentView` if they want.
The text should probably start at y=130, but... left aligned yet centered, the text should accept '\n' and render new lines correctly
The bottom of the display should say "press anywhere to continue". And that should be the behavior.
It's internal onunload should clear all of these configured parameters to avoid the next call having lingering unexpected behaviors

`RecordingView` should use this view to confirm that a recording is finished, using `sprit_thumbsup_100` as the sprite, the text saying "<file name>\nhas been recorded", before transitioning to the main view
agent_reply: Implemented `ModalDialog` as a `FlyGuiView` and hooked it into the recording completion flow.
- Registered the view and exposed `get_modal_dialog()` in [all_init.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:1)
- Configurable sprite, dimensions, text, and next view.
- Sprite is centered below the top bar.
- Text starts at `y=130`, left aligned in a centered text area, with `\n` supported.
- Touch anywhere or press any hardware button dismisses and transitions to the configured next view.
- `onUnload()` clears the configuration to avoid stale state.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest`; build passed.

user_prompt: For `ModalDialog`, the text needs to move up, the starting Y should be the ending Y of the sprite
agent_reply: Done. `ModalDialog` text now starts at the sprite’s bottom edge: `kSpriteY + spriteHeight_` in [ModalDialog.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ModalDialog.cpp:143).

user_prompt: switch out the font that ModalDialog uses, keep the size the same as much as possible. Right now, the character "c" is rendering with such a tiny gap that it looks like a "o"
agent_reply: Changed `ModalDialog` to use font `2` instead of the GLCD font:
- Body line height nudged from `16` to `17`
That should make `c` read much less like `o` without blowing up the layout.

user_prompt: Is the height of the top bar defined by one constant and shared globally? if it is not, then please tuck it in `lib\FlyGui\FlyGui.h` and have everything else use the same constant, such as the big red border drawing, the placement of sprites in the dialogs, etc
agent_reply: I added `FlyGui::kTopBarHeight` in [FlyGui.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.h:57), and kept `FlyGui::topBarHeight()` returning that value for compatibility.
I updated top-bar-dependent layout to use the shared constant, including:
- recording red border / clear area / Bluetooth icon offset
Build passed. Warnings were from existing library code, not this change.

user_prompt: when going back from a ModalDialog back into MainScreenView, the bottom yellow text is leaving a few residual free pixels
I think the right move is to make ModalDialog clear the screen (fill the region black, do not draw over the top bar) in its own onunload
how was this solved for RecordingView? it had the same problem
agent_reply: RecordingView solved the same artifact by clearing the whole content region in `onUnload()`:

I added the same pattern to [ModalDialog.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/ModalDialog.cpp:50), so dismissing a modal clears everything below the top bar before returning to `MainScreenView`.
No build run for this one-line behavioral fix.

commit_message: more gui work, auto bt reconnect

user_prompt: another thing I want, upon completion of pairing, before launching the ModalDialog, temporarily set the Bluetooth stack to non-connectable-non-discoverable, when the user dismisses the ModalDialog, go back to connectable-non-discoverable
agent_reply: I added two explicit BtManager scan-mode helpers:
Pairing success now does this flow:
4. When the dialog is dismissed, request restore to connectable/non-discoverable.
5. The restore is executed from the core-0 loop, not directly inside the GUI touch callback.
I also added an optional `ModalDialog` dismiss callback for this kind of flow.

commit_message: progress on bluetooth but cannot initiate connection, can handle incoming though

user_prompt: also make sure every `.cpp` file inside `lib\FlyGuiViews` meet the guidelines at `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md`, however, it is also important that their code layout look almost the same between them as they do kind of inherit from the same base classes sometimes
agent_reply: The common shape is now: constants/types/prototypes first, primary view/API methods next, supporting/drawing/formatting helpers later. I also kept the FlyGui view files visually aligned with each other by using the same lightweight section markers and putting helper blocks at the bottom.
- `src\version.c` build churn was restored; final status only shows the intended layout files.
